behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is handler-to-queue handoff plus auth check: reject + "not forwarded" requires wiring HTTP handler and queue publisher together. Integration-brake: no real OS/network/subprocess required (can use in-process HTTP harness), no AI output, no multi-agent chain. Rejected higher level because behavior text is only signature validation and enqueue suppression, not real network timing or infrastructure faults.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler with in-process HTTP test server/client and a spy/fake queue adapter. Send body with mismatched X-Signature-256. Assert 401 and zero enqueue calls (or no persisted queue record).

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: This is an ordering invariant across modules (HTTP handler + persistent queue write path). A unit test of one function misses real handoff ordering bugs. Integration-brake: no required real external network/subprocess; local persistent store (e.g., sqlite/file/db module) is enough; no AI; no multi-step agent flow. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real OS/network effects beyond local module wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process HTTP request plus real queue persistence module (local test DB/file). Instrument enqueue completion timestamp/marker and response completion. Assert queue write commits before 200 is observed.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Bug appears at module interaction boundary between request path and async downstream processor. Need to verify decoupling, not just single-function logic. Integration-brake: can reproduce with in-process server and delayed downstream stub; no real OS/network/subprocess dependency required; no AI; no multi-agent chain. Rejected System because behavior text is about await/non-await semantics, not real infra timing pathologies.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler with downstream processor that blocks on a controllable promise. Send valid request and assert HTTP response completes while processor promise is still unresolved, then resolve processor and confirm eventual completion side effects.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on wall-clock latency under concurrency and backlog pressure; event-loop scheduling, socket handling, and runtime contention can hide in mocks. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE both push to System. Rejected Integration because text requires "within 3 seconds wall-clock" and "slow and concurrent" conditions that are often masked by in-process fakes/time control.
behaviors.test_description: Start real Node process exposing HTTP endpoint and real queue/downstream worker configuration. Generate concurrent prior slow events, then send a new valid event and measure end-to-end HTTP latency with real clock. Assert response <3s under defined load profile.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Failure is cross-request state handoff (dedupe key check + persistent queue write) across modules; unit logic alone can miss duplicate writes caused by wiring/transaction boundaries. Integration-brake: no mandatory real network/subprocess; no AI dependence; no multi-agent steps. Rejected System because behavior text does not require OS/network effects, only persistence-level idempotency.
behaviors.test_description: Use handler + real persistent queue/dedupe store in test environment. Send same event ID twice (optionally concurrently). Assert only one persisted queue entry and second request returns expected idempotent outcome.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: This checks contract mapping across queue module -> HTTP layer (full condition propagation and response translation). Unit test of formatter alone is insufficient for real boundary failure. Integration-brake: no required real OS/network/subprocess; queue-full can be produced by in-process queue implementation/stub; no AI; no multi-step agentic flow. Rejected System because behavior text is explicit about queue signal mapping, not real infrastructure failure modes.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler with queue module configured to return/throw a deterministic "full" condition. Send request and assert HTTP 503 plus correct Retry-After header value.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: This is a queue+storage enforcement invariant; requires real queue implementation with persistence path, not just mocked responses. Integration-brake: still local DB/filesystem-level component test (no real external network/subprocess required), no AI, no multi-agent chain. Rejected System because behavior text is capacity enforcement in queue component, not OS/network timing or subprocess behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate real persistent queue with small configured capacity in a test-local store. Enqueue until capacity, then enqueue one more. Assert overflow is rejected and persisted depth never exceeds configured limit.
